markets.rules_primary: If Donald Trump has taken at least 9 presidential actions from Jul 19, 2026 through Jul 25, 2026 (as checked on the “Presidential Actions” page at whitehouse.gov at 10:00 AM ET on Jul 26, 2026), then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
The market solely resolves to the publication dates visibly displayed on the "Presidential Actions" tab at whitehouse.gov at 10:00 AM ET on Jul 26, 2026. The week runs from Jul 19, 2026 through Jul 25, 2026. The actions may include proclamations, executive orders, nominations & appointments, and memoranda. Any item listed on the page counts, regardless of its legal effect.

Examples (for Jan 10, 2026):

Executive Order with a Jan 7 signing date is released with a Jan 10 publication date → resolves Yes
Proclamation e-mailed to White House Press Pool but not uploaded by 10:00 AM next day → resolves No
Proclamation is released Jan 10 with a backdated Jan 9 publication date → resolves No
Bill signed into law and shows up on "Briefings & Statements" instead of "Presidential Actions" → resolves No

markets.rules_secondary: The observation period begins at 12:00 AM ET on Jul 19, 2026 and ends at 11:59 PM ET on Jul 25, 2026. The primary resolution source for this market is Roll Call. In the event that Roll Call data is unavailable or inaccurate, alternative sources may be used at Kalshi's discretion. Truths, ReTruths, and Quote Truths are included in the total count. Replies are included only if captured by Roll Call. The post count will be recorded at 10:00 AM ET on Jul 26, 2026. Posts deleted as of the Expiration Time will not be included. "Between" is inclusive of the lower and upper bound.
markets.title: Will Donald Trump make between 100 and 119 Truth Social posts the week of Jul 19, 2026?

markets.rules_primary: If Donald Trump endorses at least 50 people for their election on Truth Social from Jul 19, 2026 through Jul 25, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Any formal endorsement will qualify, including reaffirmations of prior endorsements. If Trump endorses multiple people in an election, all will be counted towards the total. The endorsement must be a clear, affirmative statement of support. Ambiguous statements of support will not qualify. A person cannot be counted more than once in a given week.

Examples:
Donald Trump states "I endorse [candidate] for [office]"
Donald Trump states "I support [candidate]" referencing a political race
Donald Trump states "Vote for [candidate]" publicly
Donald Trump states "[candidate] has my full support" for [race]

Examples that would NOT count:
Trump posts a graphic that says "[Candidate] wins" that merely states as fact they were endorsed by President Trump
markets.title: Will Trump endorse at least 50 people on Truth Social this week?

markets.rules_primary: If the number of distinct days Donald Trump is photographed in a tagged editorial photo on Getty Images between Jul 20, 2026 and Jul 26, 2026 is exactly 7, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
If a tagged photo does not actually depict Donald Trump, or is materially mis-tagged (for example, with the wrong date), Kalshi reserves the right to disregard that photo when resolving the market. Conversely, if a photo is correctly tagged as depicting Donald Trump but its metadata date does not reflect the actual date the photo was taken (in local time), Kalshi reserves the right to include that photo based on the actual date.

Example of a date with no qualifying photos: April 4, 2026

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if complete Federal Register data becomes available.
markets.rules_primary: If the President signs above 2 executive orders during Jul 19, 2026 to Jul 25, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
An "Executive Order" is a numbered presidential directive that: is formally titled "Executive Order" followed by a number, is signed by the President, is published in the Federal Register, and has the force of law. All documents officially designated as "Executive Orders" with sequential numbering count, including Executive Orders that are later revoked or amended. 

The signing date as recorded in the Federal Register determines which period an Executive Order belongs to. If, for example, the period was "Jan 1, 2025 to Jan 7, 2025," and on January 10th the Federal Register published a numbered Executive Order with a January 7th signing date, the market would resolve to Yes. The latest Expiration Date of the Contract shall be two weeks after the end of the period. If an Executive Order that is dated past Jul 19, 2026 to Jul 25, 2026 is posted to the Federal Register, expiration may be moved to an earlier date.
markets.title: Will the President sign more than 2 Executive Orders between Jul 19, 2026 and Jul 25, 2026?
